title: Tarn Mison

'''Tarn Mison''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Las Lagon]] who served as an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] before defecting to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] after witnessing the mass [[Death/Legends|execution]] of [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] on the celestial body [[Farbinda III]] by the Empire. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Mison was stationed at the Rebellion's [[Alliance High Command/Legends|headquarters]] of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

After serving as [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu's]] [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Mison was among a group of six [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilots]] who flew [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] escort for the [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] [[Bright Hope/Legends|''Bright Hope'']] through the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] in Hoth's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. Despite Mison's and his group's efforts, the ''Bright Hope'' failed to escape destruction during its blockade run.

==Biography==
===Imperial service and smuggling===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Tarn Mison<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from<ref name="EGTW" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref> [[Las Lagon]],<ref name="EGTW" /> located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Las Lagon system/Legends|Las Lagon system]]</ref> Growing up, Mison learned to [[Pilot/Legends|fly]], and he became noted as a talented bush pilot.<ref name="EGTW" /> At some point, Mison joined the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], serving as a pilot.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" />

[[File:ThatJerkWhoAlwaysRuinsYourPhotos-ESBHD.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Tarn Mison in Echo Base]]
Eventually, Mison turned to [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], playing his trade on the [[Shipwrights' Trace/Legends|Shipwrights' Trace]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], he accepted a job to ferry supplies to the celestial body [[Farbinda III]], where a group of conscientious objectors had established a [[Colonist/Legends|colony]]. Upon arrival, he discovered that an Imperial strike team had rounded up the local populace, who offered no resistance. Mison watched as the Imperials summarily [[Death/Legends|executed]] the colonists.<ref name="EGTW" />

Horrified, Mison spent several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] deliberating on the events before deciding to join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]<ref name="EGTW" /> shortly following<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> For a [[Time/Legends|time]], Mison served the Rebellion at [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi Station]] on the<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> prior to the Alliance's [[Evacuation of Yavin|evacuation]] of the base [[0 ABY/Legends|six months]] following the Battle of Yavin.<ref name="NEC" />

===Smuggling and Rebel Alliance service===
[[File:Rogue_Group_TESB_Manga.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The twelve airspeeders of Rogue Group, including gunner Mison, attack during the Battle of Hoth.]]
As a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], Mison earned an assignment to<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> the [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] headquarters of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on the [[Water/Legends|ice]] planet<ref name="NEC" /> [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> where he was passing through the facility's main [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] while [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Shawn Valdez]] issued orders regarding the base's evacuation procedure to his subordinates in the face of imminent Imperial invasion just prior to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]].<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

Mison was drafted into the hastily assembled [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Group]], a collection of [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]] and [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] drawn from Rogue Flight, [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], and [[Green Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Green Squadron]]. Rogue Group was assigned to fly [[Snowspeeder/Legends|snowspeeders]] to counter the Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT walker]] assault and give the Alliance forces time to evacuate the planet. Mison was partnered with [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]], serving as the gunner.<ref name="EGTW" />

===Escape from Hoth===
[[File:Tarn_Mison_X-Wing_XWM.png|thumb|right|300px|Mison's X-wing]]
Both Celchu and Mison survived the subsequent battle.<ref name="EGTW" /> During the last stages of<ref name="Of Possible Futures">{{StoryCite|story=Of Possible Futures: The Tale of Zuckuss and 4-LOM|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref> the evacuation, Mison,<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> along with pilots [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek "Hobbie" Klivian]], and [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]],<ref name="SWCL">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> was one of six Rebel pilots who flew [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] as escort for the [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]] [[Bright Hope/Legends|''Bright Hope'']], the final Rebel evacuation [[Transport/Legends|transport]] to depart the besieged Echo Base alongside<ref name="Of Possible Futures" /> the GR-75 medium [[Starship/Legends|transports]] [[Thon's Orchard/Legends|''Thon's Orchard'']]<ref name="Running the Gauntlet-GG3-2">{{WEGCite|story=Running the Gauntlet|book=Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back|reprint=1}}</ref> and [[Dutyfree/Legends|''Dutyfree'']].<ref name="Inside the Worlds">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy]]''</ref>

However, Mison's escort failed to see the ''Bright Hope'' through the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockade]] waiting in Hoth's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. A combined attack by Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and the [[Mist Hunter/Legends|''Mist Hunter'']], the craft of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]] and [[4-LOM/Legends|4-LOM]], all but destroyed the ''Bright Hope'' before it could reach the safety of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. Seeing that there was nothing they could do for the ''Bright Hope'' and that no rescue attempt could be made for any possible survivors, Mison and the five escorting [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] pilots escaped into hyperspace themselves.<ref name="Of Possible Futures" />

Ultimately, despite the damage done to the ''Bright Hope'', many of the transport's passengers managed to evacuate to safety.<ref name="Of Possible Futures" /> Likewise, ''Thon's Orchard''<ref name="Running the Gauntlet-GG3-2" /> and ''Dutyfree'' also managed to safely evacuate.<ref name="Isard's Revenge">''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Tarn Mison's interests generally revolved around [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] rather than [[Politics/Legends|politics]]. However, upon witnessing the Imperial atrocity on Farbinda III, Mison's conscience led him to join the Alliance to Restore the Republic to oppose the Empire.<ref name="EGTW" /> He had fair skin, [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], and a brown beard.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" />

==Skills and abilities==
Mison was a skilled pilot<ref name="EGTW" /> and expert marksman.<ref name="Special Edition Limited" />

==Equipment==
Mison wore the standard [[Rebel flight suit]] along with a [[Flight helmet/Legends|flight helmet]] during his service as a Rebel pilot.<ref name="ESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Yes, they got the artist's ''[sic]'' together to do a scene that was played out over a Matte painting that I had done of Rebel ships parked in the Ice cave. We were going to be Rebel soldiers ''[…]'' Joe Johnston, Harrison Ellinshaw ''[sic]'' and Mike Pangrazio, stood in the foreground and had a few lines.|Ralph McQuarrie discusses his and his colleagues' role in ''The Empire Strikes Back''|<ref name="McQuarrie">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ralphmcquarrie.com/interview/mcquarrie/interview.html|text=The Art by Ralph McQuarrie – Interview|work=ralphmcquarrie.com; "Ralph McQuarrie Illustrator" DVD|archivedate=20031027114053}}</ref>}}
[[File:McQuarrie-Ellenshaw-Pangrazio.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Michael Pangrazio and other ILM artists with cameos in ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]
Tarn Mison first appeared as a Rebel pilot extra in the [[1980]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="ESB" /> Although uncredited, [[Industrial Light & Magic]] matte artist [[Michael Pangrazio]] portrayed Mison in the film. In fact, all of the Rebel characters present in the Mison scene were ILM artists, including [[Ralph McQuarrie]], [[Joe Johnston]], and [[Harrison Ellenshaw]].<ref name="McQuarrie" /> Their scene was filmed as part of pickups at [[Industrial Light & Magic]] in [[Wikipedia:California|California]] on [[February 9]], [[1980]], and was completed in ten takes.<ref name="The Making of The Empire Strikes Back">''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>

The Mison character was first provided a name and a brief backstory on the [http://www.starwarsccg.org/wiki/index.php?title=Lieutenant_Tarn_Mison "Lieutenant Tarn Mison" card] as part of the [[1998]] ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' expansion set of [[Decipher]]'s [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. The card's information [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] Mison<ref name="Special Edition Limited" /> as being one of the X-wing pilots who escorted the ''Bright Hope'' off Hoth in the [[1996]] short story "[[Of Possible Futures: The Tale of Zuckuss and 4-LOM]]," published in ''[[Tales of the Bounty Hunters]]''.<ref name="Of Possible Futures" />

In [[2012]], [[Jason Fry]]'s reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' established Mison as the gunner of Rogue Five during the Battle of Hoth. As the book formally confirms Mison was part of Rogue Group,<ref name="EGTW" /> the character also indirectly appears in the<ref name="Empire Manga 1">[[Star Wars Manga: The Empire Strikes Back 1|''Star Wars Manga: The Empire Strikes Back'' 1]]</ref> [[January 27]], [[1999]]<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54604/star_wars_the_empire_strikes_back_manga_1999_1|text=Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back Manga (1999) #1}}</ref> [[Star Wars Manga: The Empire Strikes Back 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars Manga: The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'' manga adaptation]], which depicts all twelve of Rogue Group's [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]].<ref name="Empire Manga 1" />
